steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to describe the researchers' methodology — not the goal, not the results, but what they actually did. A methodology statement covers the experimental setup: what was tested, under what conditions, with what controls.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — J.E. López and C. Vaughan wanted to explore the effects of chestnut short-tailed bats' ingestion on Piper sancti-felicis seed germination.: Describes what the researchers hoped to find, not what they did. ✗
  • Choice B — In the study, a lower percentage of Piper sancti-felicis seeds ingested by bats germinated than those that had not been ingested by bats.: Describes an outcome, not a procedure. ✗
  • Choice C — The team of researchers tested the germination of 125 Piper sancti-felicis seeds that had been ingested by chestnut short-tailed bats and 125 Piper sancti-felicis seeds that had not been ingested.: Identifies that 125 ingested seeds and 125 non-ingested control seeds were used — exactly the experimental setup. ✓
  • Choice D — Research by J.E. López and C. Vaughan revealed that 82 of 125—that is, 66%—of the Piper sancti-felicis seeds that had been ingested by the bats germinated.: Gives a single data point from the findings, not the methodology. ✗
That matches Choice C — The team of researchers tested the germination of 125 Piper sancti-felicis seeds that had been ingested by chestnut short-tailed bats and 125 Piper sancti-felicis seeds that had not been ingested..
